key_insight: The mean inner potential (MIP) of alloys is governed mainly by two effects: linear composition dependence at constant volume and rescaling with volume change under strain.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical way to predict electrostatic potential variations in alloyed, strained, and density-varying materials for semiconductor and TEM-based analysis.
method: DFT calculations using an augmented plane waves plus local orbitals basis set
Compare MIP trends across alloy concentration and lattice-volume changes
Separate composition effects from volume/strain-driven rescaling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent trends across multiple materials: linear MIP vs concentration for AlGaAs and clear bowing for InGaP and GeSi when volume changes are included.
watch_item: The model appears empirical at the rescaling level, so accuracy may depend on whether a material follows the same volume–composition coupling as the studied systems.
paper_title: Modelling the mean inner potential of alloyed and strained materials
